TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER REGARDING THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO PROVIDE PROPER NOTICE OF ITS AMENDED MOTION BECAUSE IT FAILED TO ADD 2 ADDITIONAL DAYS FOR ELECTRONIC SERVICE PURSUANT TO GENERAL ORDER 158, C.C.P. 1010.6 AND CRC 2.251(F). EVEN IF THE PORTION OF THE MOTION SEEKING A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS CONSIDERED AS TIMELY BASED ON DEFENDANT'S INITIAL NOTICE OF JANUARY 3, IT IS DENIED BASED ON DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO DEMONSTRATE THAT IT PROPERLY MET AND CONFERRED PRIOR TO FILING THE MOTION AS REQUIRED BY C.C.P. 2025.420(A). THE PORTION OF THE AMENDED MOTION WHICH ADDED A MOTION TO STAY OR DISMISS ON THE GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS THAT WAS NOT INCLUDED IN THE INITIAL MOTION IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Derek S. Johnson of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party Pierce Engineering Company
Kimberly Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Myron Youngbluth

RULING:
DEFENDANT PIERCE ENGINEERING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Pierce Engineering Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 2 (premises owner/contractor liability) and off calendar as to issues 1, 3 and 4 (strict liability, civil battery and punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiff's claims against it. Even if defendant demonstrated that plaintiff was a sophisticated user, such defense applies only to the duty to warn and is not a complete bar to plaintiff's claims. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant breached a duty owed to him. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, Dr. Cohen's declaration would create a triable issue whether defendant breached a duty owed to plaintiff. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff has no evidence to support his premises owner/contractor liability cause of action. Defendant failed to show that such cause of action must fail even if plaintiff has no evidence that defendant exercised control over the premises and/or plaintiff's work. Plaintiff represents that he has dismissed his strict liability and civil battery causes of action and his claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION OF DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER TO FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER REGARDING THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Foster Wheeler LLC's amended motion for a protective order regarding the deposition of plaintiff is denied. Defendant failed to provide proper notice of its amended motion because it failed to add 2 additional days for electronic service pursuant to General Order 158, C.C.P. 1010.6 and CRC 2.251(f).
